messages.subject: [R-sig-Epi] survival curve in epi package
messages.body_plain:
You can have a look at the Icens() function. But that will give you
estimates of a piecewise constant hazard. You will then have to
transform this to the survival function.
______________________________________________

Bendix Carstensen
Senior Statistician
Steno Diabetes Center
Niels Steensens Vej 2-4
DK-2820 Gentofte
Denmark
+45 44 43 87 38 (direct)
+45 30 75 87 38 (mobile)
bxc at steno.dk   http://www.biostat.ku.dk/~bxc



> -----Original Message-----
> From: r-sig-epi-bounces at stat.math.ethz.ch 
> [mailto:r-sig-epi-bounces at stat.math.ethz.ch] On Behalf Of 
> Summer Nitely
> Sent: 8. februar 2008 09:37
> To: r-sig-epi at stat.math.ethz.ch
> Subject: [R-sig-Epi] survival curve in epi package
> 
>  Does anyone know if the Epi package has a function that 
> returns/plots the survival function for interval censored 
> survival analysis? I haven't found one in the manual that 
> does this, but I could be missing it.
> 
> 	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]
> 
> _______________________________________________
> R-sig-Epi at stat.math.ethz.ch mailing list 
> https://stat.ethz.ch/mailman/listinfo/r-sig-epi
>
messages.body_snippet:
You can have a look at the Icens() function. But that will give you
estimates of a piecewise constant hazard. You will then have to
transform this to the survival function.
___________________________

messages.subject: [R-sig-Epi] Course: STATISTICAL PRACTICE IN EPIDEMIOLOGY USING R
messages.subject_clean: Course: STATISTICAL PRACTICE IN EPIDEMIOLOGY USING R
messages.body_plain:
Course in
 
STATISTICAL PRACTICE IN EPIDEMIOLOGY USING R

============================================

Tartu, Estonia, Wednesday 28 May to Monday 2 June 2008.

Aimed at young statisticians and epidemiologists wishing to broaden
their epidemiological skills, in particular with respect to practical
statistical analysis. Participants will gain access to the versatile
analysis tool R which has good analytical in particular graphical 
capabilities. The Epi-package for R will be extensively used.

Participants are required to have a fairly good understanding of
statistical principles and some familiarity with epidemiological
concepts. The course will be mainly practically oriented with more
than half the time at the computer.

Price: Commercial: 1000 EUR, Academic: 750 EUR / 500 EUR.
The 750 EUR are for particpants from the EU pre-2004, North America
and the like. 500 for particants from other countries.

Application deadline: 15 April 2004.

Send an application by mail to the organizers, briefly stating your
qualifications in epidemiology and statistics and a brief reason to
participate: krista.fischer at mrc-bsu.cam.ac.uk; tilel at sun3.oulu.fi;
bxc at steno.dk

Further information at the course homepage: www.biostat.ku.dk/~bxc/SPE

------------------------------------------------------
Krista Fischer, University of Tartu, Estonia
Esa L??r?, University of Oulu, Finland
Bendix Carstensen, Steno Diabetes Center, Denmark 
(Organizers)

messages.subject: [R-sig-Epi] need help in calculating confidence intervals for sensitivity, specificity, PPV & NPV
messages.subject_clean: need help in calculating confidence intervals for sensitivity, specificity, PPV & NPV
messages.body_plain:
Hello,

I am a physician and a novice in R.

I am trying to calculate sensitivity, specificity,
positive predictive and negative predictive values. I
could do that using the package caret. However, I need
to get confidence intervals around these estimates and
I do not know how to do that.

Please help me find a way to calculate confidence
intervals for these parameters.

thank you.
Sincerely,
Dr. Smita Pakhale

messages.subject: [R-sig-Epi] need help in calculating confidence intervals forsensitivity, specificity, PPV & NPV
messages.subject_clean: need help in calculating confidence intervals forsensitivity, specificity, PPV & NPV
messages.body_plain:
The sensitivity, specificity and predictive values are just proportions,
so yoiu can use the function binom.test() to get exact confidence
confidence intervals.

Best,
Bendix

The sensitivity, specificity and predictive values are just proportions,
so yoiu can use the function binom.test() to get exact confidence
confidence intervals.

Best,
Bendix
